title: ophrys sphegodes subsp. araneola
intro: Ophrys sphegodes subsp. araneola, also known as the spider orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The spider orchid has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and improving sleep quality due to its adaptogenic properties. Herbal preparations of the spider orchid can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and salves, often used to treat skin issues and promote wound healing.
